title: Writing - Part 1: Natural Disasters
description: Your class has been discussing how communities and governments can better prepare for and respond to natural disasters.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the measures in your notes. You should explain which measure you think is most important, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Advanced technology can predict earthquakes, floods and storms with increasing accuracy, saving lives.
Local communities that practise emergency procedures regularly are far better equipped to survive disasters.
Richer nations have a moral responsibility to assist countries that lack resources to deal with major disasters.

structure.intro: Introduce the challenge of natural disasters and state the two measures you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first measure and how it helps communities prepare or respond.
structure.body2: Discuss the second measure and compare its importance to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which measure you consider most important and explain your view.

content.modelAnswer:
Natural disasters such as earthquakes, floods and hurricanes cause enormous loss of life and destruction each year. While it is impossible to prevent them, societies can take steps to minimise their impact. Two of the most valuable measures are investing in early warning systems and building community preparedness.

Early warning systems use technology to detect and forecast natural events before they strike, giving people time to evacuate or take protective action. Countries with sophisticated monitoring systems, such as Japan for earthquakes or the United States for tornadoes, have shown that timely warnings can significantly reduce casualties. As climate change increases the frequency of extreme weather, these systems are becoming ever more essential.

Community preparedness is equally vital. When people know what to do in an emergency, have access to supplies and regularly practise evacuation procedures, they are far more likely to survive a disaster and recover quickly. Building this resilience requires ongoing education and investment at the local level.

In my opinion, early warning systems represent the most critical measure, as they directly save lives by providing the crucial time needed for action. However, they are most effective when communities are already prepared to respond. Both measures work best in combination, and investment in both should be a priority for governments worldwide.
